operation: First, create a workplane on the XY coordinate plane, sketch a 30mm by 20mm rectangle, and extrude the sketch 8mm to form a solid rectangular base. Next, select the top Z-facing face of the base to set up a new workplane, sketch an ellipse with a 12mm major radius and 8mm minor radius, then extrude this ellipse 6mm upward. Finally, select the top Z-facing face of the extruded elliptical feature and perform a shell operation with a 1.5mm wall thickness, which hollows out the interior of the elliptical protrusion while leaving the base solid and retaining uniform wall thickness for the elliptical side walls.
geometry: The model is composed of a solid flat rectangular prism base and a hollow elliptical vertical protrusion attached concentrically to the top surface of the base. The elliptical protrusion is open at its top end, has consistent wall thickness, and the bottom of its hollow cavity is the flat top surface of the rectangular base. The overall structure is symmetric along the axis aligned with both the long side of the rectangle and the major axis of the ellipse.
function: This is a mountable socket base designed to hold elliptical or oval profile components. The flat rectangular base can be easily fastened to a mounting surface, while the hollow upper receptacle accepts a matching elliptical part to secure it in place, making it suitable for use as a fitting adapter or small component holder.
